claim_boundary.what_is_not_supported: This is a finite computational-basis replay inverse certificate, not a complex linear operator, Hermiticity proof, Hamiltonian sum, spectral theorem, Quantum PCP/NLTS theorem, global impossibility result, BQP separation, or quantum-advantage claim.
claim_boundary.what_is_supported: Lean checks that each Pauli factor replay is self-inverse and that any finite Pauli term followed by its reversed factor list restores the computational-basis state and accumulated plus/minus phase.

command: mkdir -p .lake/build/lib/B9/ClusterStabilizer && lake env lean -o .lake/build/lib/B9/ClusterStabilizer/WidthLocality.olean B9/ClusterStabilizer/WidthLocality.lean && lake env lean -o .lake/build/lib/B9/ClusterStabilizer/PauliBasisAction.olean B9/ClusterStabilizer/PauliBasisAction.lean && lake env lean -o .lake/build/lib/B9/ClusterStabilizer/PauliActionComposition.olean B9/ClusterStabilizer/PauliActionComposition.lean && lake env lean -o .lake/build/lib/B9/ClusterStabilizer/PauliActionCommutation.olean B9/ClusterStabilizer/PauliActionCommutation.lean && lake env lean -o .lake/build/lib/B9/ClusterStabilizer/PauliActionTermCommutation.olean B9/ClusterStabilizer/PauliActionTermCommutation.lean && lake env lean B9/ClusterStabilizer/PauliActionReverse.lean

requirements.label: Lean and Lake version probes return zero
All six B9 modules compile together
The source defines reverse factor-list order
The source checks the identity action composition boundary
Each Pauli factor action is self-inverse
The term theorem uses reverse factor order
The term proof is lifted inductively over the factor list
The checked theorem recovers the identity action and input state
Fresh transcript binds every source and project hash
The proof is finite replay semantics rather than a spectral theorem
The source contains no matrix or complex-amplitude machinery
The source does not claim Quantum PCP, NLTS, BQP, or advantage resolution
